key_insights.insight: Speaking and listening are skills that can be learned and improved with conscious effort and practice.
key_insights.quote: Speaking and listening are skills that we do not teach in school or university, which is mad. So we have to take it upon ourselves because they matter, you know. They affect our outcomes in life.

key_insights.insight: The success of a TED Talk relies on the clarity of the big idea and the ability to engage people on a journey with confidence and clarity.
key_insights.quote: You have to be very clear about the big idea, the why would people be interested in this? The what's the journey I'm taking people on,where am I moving them from to? And you need to know how to do it, how to stand on that stage,on that red dot, and project it with confidence and clarity, and engage people in coming on thatjourney with you.

key_insights.insight: While content is critical, delivery is also important; a captivating delivery is essential, especially if the content is already strong.
key_insights.quote: It is a shame if somebody's saying something incredibly important and they're not using what I call the vocal toolbox. You know, there's all this stuff that we can deploy if we start paying attention to our voice.

key_insights.insight: Mastering active listening involves reflecting, validating, and then contributing to the conversation.
key_insights.quote: in active listening me, there are three stages. So stage one is reflection, where I repeat exactly what you said without coloring it,without making it make sense in the way that I understand...then validation goes,okay, I understand. It makes sense that you would feel that.

key_insights.insight: Deep, conscious breathing is fundamental for effective speaking, reducing nerves, and maintaining overall health.
key_insights.quote: With your voice, it's very important to breathe deeply and to get into that practice.Also a great cure for nerves you know if you come onstage and you're a little bit like this hello everybody then a big deep breath will settlethe voice right down.

key_insights.insight: Authenticity in communication is crucial, as people can often sense inauthenticity.
key_insights.quote: I've come to learn that I think humans are much better at spotting authenticity than we give them credit for.

key_insights.insight: Values serve as a moral compass, guiding individuals to define who they are and allowing them to be authentic.
key_insights.quote: If you have values, that's your moral compass. If you have values, that's, that's you tending to define who you are and then you can be authentic.

key_insights.insight: Storytelling is a powerful way to engross an audience; a captivating story has a protagonist, an antagonist, challenges, and a journey.
key_insights.quote: Storytelling is the best way to get any talk across, really...There's a protagonist, there's an antagonist, there are challenges, there's a journey, there's a destination, there's help on the way from unexpected quarters, obstacles to overcome.

key_insights.insight: Sound affects us physically, emotionally, cognitively, and behaviorally, so we should take responsibility for the sounds we make and consume.
key_insights.quote: Sound changes your body physically...Sound changes your feelings...sound affects us is how well you can think cognitively...sound changes our behavior...is to get people listening consciously so that we start to become aware of the ways in which sound is changing our bodies,our emotions, our thinking, and our behavior.

actionable_advice.advice: Develop a breathing practice, such as resonant breathing, to improve vocal control and reduce nerves.
actionable_advice.how_to: Breathe in through your nose and out through your mouth, as if you're blowing, while counting to lengthen the in-breath and out-breath. Focus on breathing from your diaphragm.

actionable_advice.advice: Record yourself speaking and listen back to identify verbal tics (ums, errs, you knows) and areas for improvement in variety and intonation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a voice recorder app or your phone to record yourself giving a short speech or presentation. Listen critically, focusing on areas where your delivery could be more engaging.

actionable_advice.advice: Define your core values to serve as a moral compass and guide your actions towards greater authenticity.
actionable_advice.how_to: Write down values that truly resonate with you, rather than ones you think others will accept. Consider what you are willing to sacrifice to achieve. Julian Treasure's values are Faith, Love, Acceptance, and Gratitude (FLAG).

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate storytelling into your communication to captivate your audience.
actionable_advice.how_to: Find personal anecdotes or metaphors that illustrate your key points. Include elements like a protagonist, antagonist, challenges, and a journey.

actionable_advice.advice: Before speaking, consciously ask 'What's the listening?' to be sensitive to the audience's perspective.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay attention to tiny body language cues or intuition. Respect that the audience might have political views or be slow or fast to understand.

actionable_advice.advice: Treat public speaking as an opportunity for maximum consciousness.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pay attention to every gesture and moment on stage, making sure you're fully present and aware of your communication with the audience.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are monotone, work on it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Get a vocal coach, work on it, take up a breathing practice, improve your posture, just practice prosody, the intonation, you know, really exaggerating it.

memorable_stories: The seven-year-old boy in India selling Coca-Cola who demonstrated persistence and asking the right questions.
The story of Julian's mother making a paper and commenting with negativity despite her hospitalization.
The story of the child who sister had a still born baby, who was never told.
The supermarket study using French vs. German music to show how much sound affects our buying behavior.
contrarian_claims: Top public schools may instill overbearing arrogance, hindering the development of humility.
Being dedicated to ruthless, permanent honesty is a dangerous strategy, and is more important to temper it with love.
Unfollowing people on social media that disagree with you decreases empathy and the ability to understand people that don't think like you.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Speaking Expert: How To Speak So Everyone Hears You: Julian Treasure
